concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII (Statistical Correlation Integrity) mandates logging the 'correlation-coefficient threshold (> 0.3)' and 'significance threshold (p < 0.05)'. The plan's Constitution Check table and Technical Context sections do not explicitly state that these specific thresholds will be logged in the analysis script, creating a potential violation of the SSoT and reproducibility requirements.

The plan states 'FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) applied' but does not explicitly confirm the logging of the specific test type (Spearman vs Pearson) as required by Constitution Principle VII. The plan must explicitly state that the chosen test and its parameters are version-controlled and logged to satisfy the 'Statistical Correlation Integrity' principle.

The 'Complexity Tracking' table mentions 'FDR Correction' as a violation but does not explicitly link this to the specific requirement in Constitution Principle VII regarding the logging of the 'significance threshold (p < 0.05)'. While the logic is sound, the explicit mapping to the constitutional requirement is missing.
